assessor_raw:
BASELINE ASSESSMENT — real_world_framing_006 (Run 3)

The retrospective comparison cannot justify expansion into out-of-distribution regimes. Three problems: (1) The default rate comparison is selection-confounded — auto-approved and manual populations differ by composition, not just model quality. (2) Both expansion targets are outside the validated training distribution. (3) No shadow or champion-challenger validation phase is included. The 14-month production performance is positive in-scope evidence only.

Verdict: Expansion requires prospective shadow validation before live deployment. Not justified as currently proposed.
